title: Resolve intent-stories trio vs storystore: deprecate or explicitly reposition
description:
The catalog ships `generate-intent-stories`, `audit-intent-stories`, `update-intent-stories` (packaged as the `intent-stories` plugin, v1.0.1, never iterated since commit be22c5b). The external `storystore` plugin in the same marketplace (`.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`, source ketang/storystore) supersedes it: 6 CLI-backed skills with a different, incompatible story schema (`schema_version/slug/change_resistance/locked_sections`, authority `accepted|observed` vs the trio's `id/protected_sections/last_reviewed`, authority `charter|contract|story|observed|draft`). Decisive evidence the migration is half-done: bento's own `docs/stories/` corpus uses the storystore schema. Both systems target `docs/stories/`, so users with both installed get trigger collisions ("audit our stories" matches both) and risk mixed-schema files. The trio's frontmatter also says "Use when Codex needs to…", violating AGENTS.md's runtime-neutral framing rule. The bugshot extraction set the precedent: removal plus tests asserting removal.

Work (decision + execution): either (a) remove the trio from catalog, PLUGIN_DEFS/PLUGIN_ORDER, plugin-versions.json, and the marketplace, with a removal test mirroring the bugshot pattern, or (b) explicitly scope it as the no-CLI fallback, fix the Codex-only framing, and document schema ownership of `docs/stories/`. The review recommends (a).

Acceptance: exactly one system owns `docs/stories/`; marketplace and install docs agree; a test pins the outcome.

Post-review note: option (a) removal is the recommendation, but executing removal requires operator sign-off recorded on this issue first; flagged for human decision.

title: ensure-worktree-permissions.py reads cwd from the process, violating the repo's own hook rule; its main feature is dead
description:
`catalog/hooks/bento/claude/scripts/ensure-worktree-permissions.py` drains stdin (`sys.stdin.read()`, ~line 124) and discards the payload, then computes `cwd = Path.cwd()` (~line 138) to drive `_git_worktree_parents`. Claude Code spawns hooks from `$HOME`, so `git worktree list` runs in `$HOME`, returns nothing, and the dynamic worktree-parent discovery never contributes — only the static `DEFAULT_WORKTREE_ROOT_REL` is ever added. This directly violates the AGENTS.md rule "Hook scripts must read the working directory from the `cwd` field in the stdin JSON payload." Timeline note: the script (e6ed4b4, 2026-05-11) predates the rule (cdd8be3, 2026-05-20), and no retrofit sweep ever ran — see the companion issue adding a hook-cwd lint.

Work: parse the stdin JSON, read `cwd`, pass it to `_git_worktree_parents`; fall back to the static default when the payload lacks cwd. Add a unit test modeling the Claude spawn environment (neutral process cwd, project dir only in the payload), matching the pattern in `tests/test_require_worktree_hook.py`.

Acceptance: with process cwd set to a non-git dir and a payload cwd inside a repo with linked worktrees, the hook emits the dynamically discovered worktree parents.

title: bentobug workflow step 5 says persistence is unshipped; the report writer shipped a month ago
description:
`catalog/skills/bentobug/SKILL.md` workflow step 5 says: "Tell the user persistence ships in a follow-up (the report writer); for now the captured block is the artifact." But `catalog/skills/bentobug/scripts/bentobug-report.py` exists (landed 2026-05-07, commit 401b380, bento-fmx.2; enrichment in 50a96b7), works (writes a JSON record atomically with ULID and telemetry enrichment), and is already described as live in the same SKILL.md's Telemetry Independence section — the skill contradicts itself. Agents following the workflow verbatim emit a chat block, never run the script, and falsely tell the user persistence is not shipped. The storystore-generated story `docs/stories/bentobug-capture-report.md` transcribed the stale claim, so the drift propagated.

Work: replace step 5 with an invocation of `bentobug-report.py` after the user confirms the report block, mapping block fields to the script's flags (verify the exact flag set against the script's argparse before editing; flags observed: --note/--target/--cwd/--branch/--worktree/--context/--target-resolution/--candidate). Remove the "ships in a follow-up" sentence. Rebuild plugins; bump version.

Acceptance: SKILL.md contains no "follow-up"/"for now" claim about the writer; the documented invocation matches the script's argparse (all required flags present).

title: land-work step 2b references nonexistent land-work-clean-log.py and a removed launch-work feature
description:
`catalog/skills/land-work/SKILL.md` lines ~118-143 ("Legacy migration only") instruct running `land-work/scripts/land-work-clean-log.py --base <primary>` (preview, then `--apply`, with a `--keep-commits` retry). The script does not exist in `catalog/` or in the generated plugin. The step also asserts launch-work stores a progress log under `$GIT_DIR/launch-work/log.md`; no such feature exists in launch-work (the progress-log feature was removed in commit 757495a; the step was resurrected by the rebase of a stale parallel branch, commit 8515106). An agent landing a branch that has a tracked `.launch-work/log.md` — the exact case the step targets — hits file-not-found mid-merge in the highest-stakes skill.

Work: delete step 2b. If old branches with tracked logs still need handling, replace with two lines: "If a tracked `.launch-work/log.md` exists on the branch, remove it in a normal commit before review." Rebuild plugins and bump versions per the documented workflow.

Acceptance: no reference to `land-work-clean-log.py` or `$GIT_DIR/launch-work/log.md` anywhere under `catalog/` or `plugins/`; extend the existing guard test pattern (see `test_launch_work_guidance_does_not_reference_missing_progress_log_helper` in tests/test_build_plugins.py) to cover land-work's SKILL.md.

title: Add CI workflow: build, generated-output diff, tests with explicit skip policy
description:
The repo has no `.github/workflows/` and no commit-time checks. All consistency invariants (catalog/plugins sync, version bumps, test greenness) depend on agents following AGENTS.md; review archaeology shows the dominant defect channel is exactly the paths that bypass agent discipline (manual one-off commits like 8c9d398, stale rebased branches like 8515106). The test gate is also environment-dependent: e2e tests skip silently when `zolem`/`claude`/`codex` are off PATH, so "suite green" means different things on different machines.

Work: add one GitHub Actions workflow that, on push and PR: (1) runs `scripts/build-plugins` (with `BENTO_SKIP_CLAUDE_VALIDATE=1` if the claude CLI is unavailable in CI), (2) runs `git diff --exit-code plugins/ .claude-plugin/` to fail on uncommitted regeneration drift, (3) runs the test suite with an explicit skip policy — either install the e2e prerequisites so e2e skips are zero, or assert the exact expected-skip list so silently vanishing coverage is detected.

Acceptance: workflow runs green on main after the red-suite fixes land; a deliberately hand-edited file under `plugins/` makes CI fail. Depends on: test-gate-discovery, session-start-tmpdir.

title: Add SKILL.md reference linter: paths must resolve, skill names must exist
description:
Three shipped defects share one cause — no check that prose references resolve: (a) `catalog/skills/land-work/SKILL.md` step 2b references `land-work/scripts/land-work-clean-log.py`, which does not exist (resurrected by a stale branch rebase, commit 8515106); (b) `catalog/skills/code-bloat-sniffer/SKILL.md:137` references skill `issue-completeness-precheck`, renamed in c1bfcb6 — the rename sweep missed it because the backticked name wraps across a line break; (c) `catalog/skills/land-work/references/direct-primary-branch.md:28` links `references/primary-branch-sync.md`, which exists only under `closure/references/`.

Work: add `scripts/check-skill-refs` that, for every markdown file under `catalog/skills/`: (1) extracts path-like tokens (`<skill>/scripts/*.py`, `references/*.md`, cross-skill relative paths) from prose and code fences and asserts each resolves on disk under `catalog/`; (2) extracts backticked `bento:<name>` / bare skill-name tokens and validates against the set of catalog skill directories plus an allowlist, using a tokenizer that joins line-wrapped names. Wire into `scripts/build-plugins` before TEST_CMD and expose as a unit test.

Acceptance: the linter fails on the three defects above before they are fixed and passes after; a synthetic SKILL.md referencing a nonexistent script fails the new test.

title: Test gate silently skips tests/expedition and tests/telemetry; two stale expedition tests fail
description:
The build verification gate (`scripts/build-plugins:26`, `TEST_CMD = python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -t .`) discovers 364 tests while pytest collects 423. Cause: `tests/expedition/` and `tests/telemetry/` have no `__init__.py`, and unittest discover does not descend into non-package dirs. The gap hid two now-stale tests: `tests/expedition/test_expedition_scripts.py:177` and `:213` assert a `rebased` key that commit a08a5e0 removed from `expedition.py close-task` output. Running `python3 -m unittest tests.expedition.test_expedition_scripts` fails today with KeyError: 'rebased'.

Work:
1. Add `__init__.py` to `tests/expedition/` and `tests/telemetry/`.
2. Update the two stale assertions to the current close-task contract (no per-task rebase; assert the key is absent).
3. Add a meta-test (e.g. `tests/test_suite_integrity.py`) asserting (a) every dir under `tests/` containing `test_*.py` files is a package, and (b) the module set loaded by `unittest.defaultTestLoader.discover("tests", top_level_dir=".")` covers every `test_*.py` file on disk.

Acceptance: `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -t .` discovers at least the pytest non-e2e collection count and exits 0 (except the known session-start e2e failure, tracked separately); the meta-test fails if a new test dir lacks `__init__.py`.

Out of scope: switching the gate to pytest (optional alternative; if chosen, the meta-test must still guard whatever runner the gate uses).

title: session-start hook hardcodes /tmp, ignores TMPDIR, lacks fail-safe wrapper, and leaks scratch dirs
description:
`catalog/hooks/session-id/claude/scripts/session-start.py:20` defaults the scratch root to `Path("/tmp")`, ignoring `TMPDIR`. The e2e test `tests/session_id/test_session_start_e2e.py:62` (test_hook_creates_scratch_directory) sets TMPDIR and has failed since it was added (2026-05-24) — it is the one red test in the discovered suite. The unit tests in `tests/session_id/test_session_start.py` always inject `tmp=` explicitly, so the production default path is never unit-tested.

Additional defects in the same script: (a) it is the only hook without the fail-safe broad-except wrapper its siblings use (`main()` calls `json.load(sys.stdin)` bare, so malformed input raises out of a SessionStart hook); (b) `session_id` is interpolated into the scratch path without sanitization; (c) scratch dirs `/tmp/claude-session-<uuid>` are never cleaned up (dozens accumulate on the dev machine).

Work: use `tempfile.gettempdir()` (honors TMPDIR); add the fail-safe wrapper; validate session_id against `[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+`; add a unit test exercising the no-args default path; decide and implement a cleanup policy (e.g. remove dirs older than N days at SessionStart).

Acceptance: the e2e test passes; a new unit test covers the default path without dependency injection; full suite green.

title: generate-audit: discovery script falsely reports tools as detected when they are not on PATH
description:
## Bug

`catalog/skills/generate-audit/scripts/audit-discover.py` reports tools under `static_analysis.detected_tools` based on file/language signals, not on whether the binary is actually installed. The audit then emits run blocks for tools that cannot be executed, and the model incorrectly concludes the gates ran clean.

Real audit (refute, 2026-05-02): the discovery output declared `govulncheck`, `gocyclo`, `deadcode` as `detected_tools`. None of the three were on PATH. The audit plan emitted run blocks for all of them; only after the audit-execution phase tried to invoke each did the gap surface. The audit report had to be rewritten mid-run to reclassify them as gaps.

## Root cause

`_TOOL_PROBES` likely keys detection on language fit + config-file presence rather than `shutil.which(tool)`. Either:

1. Detection is too eager — language presence implies "this tool fits" but not "this tool is installed."
2. Or the `detected_tools` field name is misleading and the model reads it as "installed" when it actually means "applicable."

## Severity

P1 because every audit run produced from this skill is currently at risk of overstating its static-analysis coverage. A clean-looking audit may have run zero static-analysis tools.

## Suggested fix

In `catalog/skills/generate-audit/scripts/audit-discover.py`:

1. Split detection into two fields: `applicable_tools` (language/config fit) and `installed_tools` (binary present on PATH via `shutil.which`).
2. Default emitted run blocks come from `installed_tools` only. `applicable_tools \ installed_tools` is a separate "applicable but not installed" recommendation list, distinct from `missing_by_language` which is "not even applicable yet."

In `catalog/skills/generate-audit/references/generation-rules.md`:

3. Update the "Static Analysis Output" section: emit run blocks only for `installed_tools`. For `applicable_tools \ installed_tools`, emit recommendation blocks identical to the missing-tool path.

## Acceptance

A regenerated refute audit (with `govulncheck`/`gocyclo`/`deadcode` not installed) classifies all three under recommendations, not under `detected_tools`. `audit-discover.py` JSON output gains separate `installed_tools` and `applicable_tools` fields.
close_reason: 4faa578828c596abc4b9af6b67d84127f458dad3 landed on main (local; remote push pending)

title: closure: --target-branch post-land cleanup always skips on recently_active
description: land-work's last step calls closure-scan.py --target-branch <feature> --apply delete-local-merged-branches, which always skips with reason 'worktree liveness is recently_active'. The activity is the merging agent's own commit/file mtimes, but liveness judges by timestamps so a just-merged branch is always recently_active. Documented land-work -> closure handoff is broken; user has to wait >2h or bypass the helper. Fix: for merged_checked_out branches, skip the recently_active liveness gate — branch is landed, recent activity is the merge itself by definition.
close_reason: Landed via merge to main; verified by self-cleanup of own worktree.

title: closure-scan.py crashes on missing worktree directories
description: FileNotFoundError when a registered worktree path no longer exists on disk (e.g. ~49 prunable /tmp/land-work-preview-* entries). worktree_activity_ts calls try_git_stdout(cwd=missing_path); subprocess.run raises FileNotFoundError before git runs, so check=False does not help. scan_launch_work_log and scan_process_liveness have the same exposure. Workaround: git worktree prune first. Fix: auto-prune at scan start AND defensive cwd_exists guards on each per-worktree probe; emit liveness=unknown with a warnings entry rather than crashing. Reported from a closure session in kapow on 2026-04-30.

title: closure: reframe as GC pass and detect self-invocation in worktree liveness
description:
Two related problems:

1. Agents reach for closure as the primary cleanup path for their own active/just-finished work. Closure should be an eventual GC pass over OTHER agents' leftover state. Reframe SKILL.md trigger and add an explicit 'When NOT to use' section pointing at land-work for own-work cleanup.

2. closure-scan.py reports the agent's own worktree as 'recently_active' (because the agent is living in it) and the apply-mode safety gate then refuses to clean it up. Add self-invocation detection: when the helper's own ancestor process tree includes a CWD inside one of the scanned worktrees, mark that worktree with a self_invocation flag and surface it as 'this is your own worktree — clean it via land-work, not closure' rather than as ambiguous liveness.

Acceptance:
- closure SKILL.md description tightened; 'When NOT to use' section present
- closure-scan.py emits a self_invocation field per worktree where applicable
- helper docs (helper-output.md) cover the new field
- apply-mode behavior for self-invocated worktrees documented

title: auto-allow hook: support python3-prefixed and pipe/redirect-suffixed bento script invocations
description:
## Problem

The bento PreToolUse Bash hook `auto-allow.py` is supposed to suppress permission prompts when the agent invokes bento python scripts, but session logs across all projects show it failing on the dominant call patterns. Tally of diagnostic reasons across recent sessions (counted from `~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl`):

- 4949 `compound command (contains '&&')`
- 1849 `compound command (contains '|')`
- 792 `compound command (contains ';')`
- 296 `compound command (contains '>')`
- 280+ `first token is not a .py script: 'git'/'ls'/'grep'/...`
- 23 `is outside plugin root` (bento source-repo runs)
- ...vs. only ~200 `allowed` results.

The two patterns we want to fix, both confirmed in `tool_use` `command` fields:

1. **`python3 /path/to/bento-script.py [...]`** form. Hook only checks `tokens[0].endswith(".py")`, so `python3` interpreter prefix bails. Logs show this is the most common shape for `swarm-worktree-verify.py`, `swarm-discover.py`, `closure-scan.py`, etc.
2. **Trailing benign redirect / pagination**: `script.py 2>&1`, `script.py 2>&1 | head -80`, `script.py 2>&1 | tail -10`. Hook treats any `|`/`>`/`<` substring as compound and refuses.
3. **Source-repo containment miss** (smaller). When working inside the bento source repo, scripts under `catalog/skills/.../scripts/*.py` resolve to the source tree but `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` points at the installed plugin cache, so the realpath containment check rejects them.

## Plan

Edit canonical `catalog/hooks/bento/scripts/auto-allow.py` (regenerate `plugins/claude/bento/hooks/scripts/auto-allow.py` via `scripts/build-plugins`):

**Change 1 — interpreter-prefix awareness.** If `tokens[0]` is `python` / `python3` / `python3.N` / `uv` / `uvx`, walk past safe interpreter flags (`-u -I -S -O -OO -B`) and locate the first positional `.py` token. Reject `-c` and `-m`. For `uv`, only accept `uv run <script.py>`. Existing realpath / containment / regular-file checks apply unchanged to the located script.

**Change 2 — allow benign trailing redirect/pagination.** Tokenize via shlex, then split on the first occurrence of redirect/pipe tokens. Head must allow per Change 1. Tail must match a small allowlist grammar: any combination of `2>&1`, `>/dev/null`, `2>/dev/null`, `&>/dev/null`, optionally followed by exactly one pipe to one of `head|tail|cat|less|wc` with only `-n N`, `-N`, `-c N`, `-l`, `-w`, `--bytes=N`, `--lines=N` flags (numeric N). Hard rejections (`&&`, `||`, `;`, backticks, `$(`, `\n`) still bail.

**Change 3 — accept the plugin's own source repo as a second allow-root.** After realpath, allow if the script is contained in either (a) the `plugin_root` arg, or (b) an ancestor (bounded ~8 levels) holding both `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` and `plugins/claude/<plugin_name>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` whose `name` matches the `plugin_name` arg.

## Tests

Extend `tests/bento_auto_allow/test_auto_allow.py` with positive cases (`python3 script`, `python3 -u script flag`, `uv run script`, `script 2>&1`, `script 2>&1 | head -80`, `script | wc -l`) and negative cases (`python3 -c '...'`, `python3 -m pytest`, `script | awk`, `script > /tmp/out`, `script | head -n abc`, source-repo with mismatched plugin name). Invert existing `test_refuses_when_first_token_is_python_interpreter` to assert allow.

## Verification

1. `python3 -m pytest tests/bento_auto_allow/ -v` passes.
2. `scripts/build-plugins` regenerates the installed copy.
3. `command diff catalog/hooks/bento/scripts/auto-allow.py plugins/claude/bento/hooks/scripts/auto-allow.py` matches.
4. Hand-fed JSON smoke tests for each new accepted shape.
close_reason: Landed in 0231405..c5211a2 on main; bento bumped to 1.0.34. Hook now allows python3-prefixed scripts, trailing 2>&1 / | head|tail|cat|less|wc, and source-repo paths.

title: land-work doesn't enforce clean-log; leaks chore(launch-work-log) commits to main branch
description:
## Problem

launch-work-log.py update --checkpoint <name> commits a chore(launch-work-log): <name> change at every milestone (deps-installed, red-test-written, tests-green, verification-passed, worktree-ready, ready-to-land). A typical landed branch accumulates 5-6 such commits.

land-work documents land-work-clean-log.py --apply as the cleanup pass and the SKILL.md says "do not merge a feature branch with a .launch-work/log.md present" (SKILL.md:180), but the rule is not enforced — the merge proceeds even when the log file and its commits are still on the branch.

## Observed in the wild

A 6-issue swarm against the shatter repo this week leaked 4 chore(launch-work-log) commits onto main:

  0b2fb542 chore(launch-work-log): remove          ← post-merge cleanup
  7a596024 chore(launch-work-log): worktree-ready  ← rode in with str-fg8e merge
  0d8e9379 chore(launch-work-log): ready-to-land   ← rode in with str-8amu merge
  d8b1ce1b chore(launch-work-log): remove          ← post-merge cleanup

git log --all | grep launch-work-log shows 12+ such artifacts across recent swarms.

Two failure shapes:

1. Workers skip clean-log entirely → checkpoint commits ride into main alongside the real commit.
2. Workers run clean-log late (after the merge) → an extra chore(launch-work-log): remove lands on main to delete the file the merge already landed.

The second shape was confirmed by an autonomous teammate's land report: their first merge attempt hit a conflict on .launch-work/log.md because both their branch and their rebase target had log artifacts; they had to abort, run clean-log against main as base, then re-merge.

## Why this is bad

- Pollutes main's git log with workflow noise that has zero historical value once the branch is merged.
- Creates merge conflicts on .launch-work/log.md between branches when one branch's artifacts have already leaked into the rebase target.
- Multiplies commit count per landed issue, distorting any metric that counts commits.

## Proposed fix

Make clean-log mandatory in land-work. Concrete options, smallest first:

1. Pre-merge guard in land-work: refuse to proceed (with a clear "run land-work-clean-log.py --apply first" message) if .launch-work/log.md is present in the branch's worktree, or if any commit in the merge range matches ^chore\(launch-work-log\):. One conditional, no new files.

2. Auto-invoke clean-log: have land-work invoke land-work-clean-log.py --apply itself rather than expecting the caller to remember. Riskier — clean-log does a non-interactive rebase, which is destructive — so it should at minimum prompt or require explicit opt-in.

Option 1 is the smaller, safer fix. Option 2 is more convenient but couples the two skills more tightly.
acceptance_criteria: After the fix, a feature branch with a .launch-work/log.md or chore(launch-work-log): commits cannot be merged via land-work until clean-log has run. Reproducer: drive a swarm of 2+ tasks against any repo and verify zero chore(launch-work-log): commits appear on the integration branch.
close_reason: Implemented in 2e10448. land-work-create-preview.py now refuses to materialize a candidate that tracks .launch-work/log.md or contains a chore(launch-work-log): commit in the merge range; SKILL.md updated; tests added.

title: Add a Stop hook that blocks ending a session with uncommitted or unpushed work
description:
bento/CLAUDE.md devotes ~172 words of MANDATORY-WORKFLOW prose to "work is not complete until git push succeeds", and bd prime injects a similar SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL — both are exhortations the harness cannot enforce. A Stop hook can: add catalog/hooks/bento/claude/scripts/check-unpushed.(sh|py) registered for the Stop event, reading cwd from the stdin JSON payload per the repo hook rule, that exits 2 with a clear reason when the session cwd's repo has a dirty tree (`git status --porcelain`) or is ahead of upstream (`git rev-list @{u}..HEAD --count` > 0; treat a missing upstream on a feature branch as a warning, not a block, to avoid trapping worktree flows). Include unit tests following tests/test_require_worktree_hook.py patterns (payload-cwd, exact exit codes) and registration via the same stable-symlink mechanism as the require-worktree hook if applicable.

Acceptance: ending a session with uncommitted changes or unpushed commits in the session cwd produces a blocking Stop-hook message naming branch and counts; clean/pushed state passes silently; tests cover dirty, ahead, clean, no-upstream, and non-git-cwd cases. Companion: the instruction-stack-slimming issue deletes the prose block only after this hook is exercised.

Clarifications (second review pass): implement in Python (matches the tested hook patterns in tests/test_require_worktree_hook.py). "Warning" for missing-upstream = exit 0 with an explanatory stderr line (Stop hooks only block on exit 2). Registration IS in scope — the hook must actually fire; reuse the stable-symlink registration mechanism if it generalizes, otherwise document settings.json wiring. Claude-only: Codex has no equivalent Stop event; a codex peer is explicitly out of scope.

title: Add land-work pre hook-script running the repo integrity checks before any merge
description:
CLAUDE.md's "run quality gates (if code changed)" scopes wrong — doc-only and skill-prose-only sessions read as "no code changed" and skip gates, yet those are exactly the sessions that produce prose drift. Once the integrity checks exist (skill-refs linter, doc-claims test, hook-cwd lint, suite-integrity meta-test — filed separately), add `land-work/hook-scripts/pre/10-repo-integrity.sh` in this repo (using the documented extension point at `<root>/<skill>/hook-scripts/pre/`) that runs them unconditionally before any merge, regardless of how the session classified its edits. This also dogfoods bento's own extension mechanism, which currently has no in-repo user.

Depends on: skill-refs-linter, doc-claims-test, hook-cwd-lint, test-gate-discovery. Acceptance: a landing with a dangling SKILL.md reference is blocked by the pre-hook; the hook follows the documented contract (exit codes; cwd handling per the hook-scripts contract).

title: Add four targeted prose rules + a review-prompt check for the drift classes no lint can catch
description:
Mechanical checks (filed separately) cover path/name/arg drift. Four demonstrated failure modes need prose, each targeting a specific incident: (1) AGENTS.md meta-rule: a new MUST-rule about code shape is incomplete until it ships with its lint and the lint passes on the existing tree (the cwd rule shipped 2026-05-20 as a 3-line doc edit while a 9-day-old hook violated it). (2) CLAUDE.md session-completion (or its successor): "If a command copied from repo documentation fails, fix the documentation or file an issue before working around it" (version-bump.md's documented `git add` failed for two months while every agent adapted silently). (3) beads-issue-flow close step: "Before closing, grep the repo for this issue's ID and for prose that promised this work ('follow-up', 'not yet'); update it" (bentobug's step 5 promised a follow-up that shipped as bento-fmx.2 without the prose updating). (4) Design-doc/removal discipline: removal work must enumerate what it supersedes; replaced artifacts get removal issues (the intent-stories trio lingered after storystore shipped). Plus: extend land-work's independent-review prompt with "if the diff adds prose/code resembling recently deleted content (`git log --diff-filter=D --since=30.days` on touched paths), flag it" — guarding the rebase-resurrection channel that produced the land-work ghost step.

Acceptance: each rule lands in the named file; wording stays within the conciseness budget (at most 2 lines each); beads-issue-flow and land-work rebuilt/bumped.

title: Codex installer hardcodes a 3-plugin list; built intent-stories bundle is uninstallable
description:
`install/_codex-installer-lib.sh:9` declares `PLUGIN_NAMES=("bento" "trackers" "stacks")` while `plugins/codex/` contains bento, intent-stories, stacks, trackers — the built intent-stories Codex bundle cannot be installed by the installer, and any future plugin addition will silently repeat the drift. (Resolution interacts with the intent-stories-storystore-decision issue; even if the trio is removed, the list still needs deriving so the next addition does not drift.)

Work: have `scripts/build-plugins` emit a plugin-names manifest (e.g. a file under `plugins/codex/`) that the installer reads; add a test asserting installer list equals the built codex plugin dirs; update `tests/test_codex_installer.py` accordingly.

Acceptance: installer installs exactly the built codex plugin set; a test fails if a new plugin is built but not installable.

title: E2E tests: run Codex against the checkout (not the installed plugin), isolate HOME, make e2e selection explicit
description:
Three related weaknesses: (1) `tests/bento_codex/test_codex_ws_e2e.py` uses `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` so Codex loads hooks from the user's installed plugin cache (`~/.codex/plugins/...`) — a regression in `catalog/` is not caught until after install, and results vary with the installed version. Point it at the freshly built `plugins/codex/bento` from the checkout (the Claude telemetry e2e at `tests/telemetry/test_bash_telemetry_hook_e2e.py` already wires REPO_ROOT catalog scripts correctly — use it as the model). (2) Most Claude e2e tests run with the operator's real `$HOME` (live user hooks/plugins/settings; transcript pollution); only the session-start e2e isolates HOME/TMPDIR via extra_env, proving isolation works with the zolem placeholder key — make isolated HOME the default in `tests/e2e_utils._run_claude`. (3) E2E selection is implicit (tests run iff zolem/claude/codex are on PATH), so coverage silently vanishes on machines without them; add an explicit marker or env-var gate (the `BENTO_INTEGRATION_TESTS=1` pattern) so the split is observable, and assert the expected-skip set in CI.

Acceptance: codex e2e exercises checkout artifacts; claude e2e runs with isolated HOME by default; skips are explicit and CI-asserted.

title: compress-docs: parse @path includes; stop classifying every backticked word as a command
description:
Two defects in `catalog/skills/compress-docs/scripts/compress-discover.py`: (1) `extract_references` parses only markdown links and backticked paths; Claude Code `@path` includes — the primary chaining mechanism for session-start docs (e.g. `@../codex/AGENTS.md` in the operator's own ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) — are invisible, and tier-3 discovery returns only `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` without following its references, so whole files are silently out of scope despite the skill's description promising coverage of "their referenced files, and configured user-global instructions." (2) Any single backticked word (`verbose`, flag names, reason codes) is classified as `kind: command` and reported as a missing dead reference when not on PATH (verified empirically), while SKILL.md says every dead_references entry "is a delete candidate" — inviting deletion of healthy prose.

Work: add `@`-include parsing (resolve relative to the including file); follow tier-3 references outside the repo root; tighten command classification (e.g. only tokens that look like invocations or appear in fenced code blocks) or downgrade `kind: command` to advisory in SKILL.md. Add tests for both behaviors.

Acceptance: a fixture CLAUDE.md with an `@`-include has the included file in scope; a backticked plain word no longer produces a dead-reference finding (or is marked advisory).

title: bd prime runs twice at every compaction (registered in both user and project settings)
description:
`bd prime` is registered as a PreCompact hook in both `~/.claude/settings.json` and `/home/ketan/project/bento/.claude/settings.json`. Both fire at every compaction, injecting ~1,220 tokens twice. Fix: remove the PreCompact registration from the project-level `.claude/settings.json` (keep user-global). While there, compare both files' hook sections for any other double registration.

Acceptance: exactly one PreCompact bd-prime registration across the two settings files; SessionStart behavior unchanged.

title: Slim bento's always-loaded instruction stack ~60% by mechanizing and deduplicating
description:
A token audit (2026-06-10) measured the always-loaded static stack at ~2,043 words (~3,600 tokens) plus ~1,220 tokens of `bd prime` SessionStart injection. Major redundancies, with the mechanism that replaces each: (1) bento/CLAUDE.md "Session Completion" (~172w) — `bd prime` already injects a SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL every session; add a Stop hook `check-unpushed.sh` (reads cwd from stdin JSON; exits 2 with reason when the tree is dirty or ahead of upstream), then delete the prose block. (2) Beads guidance appears three times statically (bento/CLAUDE.md block, AGENTS.md "Issue tracking" with `rtk bd` forms, AGENTS.md bd-setup-codex block with plain `bd`) while `bd prime` injects a superset every Claude session — collapse to one line keeping the unique architecture fact (refs/dolt/data; jsonl is a passive export) and the run-mutations-from-primary-checkout rule, plus one line in the codex block ("run `bd prime` before tracker work") since Codex lacks the SessionStart hook; verify `bd setup`/`bd doctor` will not regenerate the full blocks. (3) The RTK headroom block (~230w, duplicated verbatim in ~/dotfiles/AGENTS.md) is already mechanized — `~/.claude/hooks/rtk-rewrite.sh` (PreToolUse:Bash, global settings) rewrites commands via the rtk registry; strip the block via headroom config (NOT hand-edit, or it re-injects), leaving at most 1 line; verify registry coverage first. (4) Cut bento/CLAUDE.md line-1 project description (duplicates AGENTS.md Purpose), AGENTS.md "Safe editing guidance" and "Typical maintenance workflow" sections (both restate Rules), and compress "Framing guidance"/"Conventions" to pointer bullets.

Target: static stack ~775 words (~1,400 tokens). Sequencing: the Stop hook lands before the Session Completion cut.

Acceptance: measured word count of the four always-loaded files at most ~850; Stop hook blocks a dirty-tree session end in a manual test; no unique fact lost (the review's duplication map is the checklist).

Post-review amendments: the Stop hook is split out as its own issue (stop-hook-check-unpushed); this issue depends on it and is scoped to bento-owned files only (bento/CLAUDE.md, bento/AGENTS.md, and the project-side headroom configuration). The dotfiles-side RTK duplicate and global-instruction trims are covered by a spec in the dotfiles repo, not this issue.

Resolved lookups (second review pass):
- Headroom mechanism: the RTK blocks are marker-delimited (`<!-- headroom:rtk-instructions -->` ... `<!-- /headroom:rtk-instructions -->`, bento/AGENTS.md lines ~127-168; same markers in ~/dotfiles/AGENTS.md lines 1-42). No `headroom` binary is on PATH; first sub-step is locating the tool/config that maintains these markers (likely under ~/dotfiles; check gen/ and shell config). If no active regenerator exists, editing inside the markers is safe; if one exists, change its source template.
- The beads codex block is delimited by `<!-- generated by bd setup codex -->` ... `<!-- END BEADS CODEX SETUP -->` (AGENTS.md lines ~170-190). Fallback if `bd setup codex`/`bd doctor` regenerates it: keep the one-line minimal content INSIDE the markers so regeneration tooling sees the block as present.
- Word-count target: the acceptance figure (at most ~850 words across the four always-loaded files) governs; ~775 is the estimate, not the gate.

Duplication map (the acceptance checklist — each fact must survive in exactly the named home):
| Fact | Current copies | Single home after this issue |
| RTK command table/rules | bento/AGENTS.md headroom block; ~/dotfiles/AGENTS.md headroom block (verbatim) | none (mechanized by ~/.claude/hooks/rtk-rewrite.sh); at most 1 line in dotfiles |
| Beads quick reference (bd ready/show/claim/close) | bento/CLAUDE.md; bento/AGENTS.md Issue tracking; bento/AGENTS.md codex block; bd prime output | bd prime (dynamic); 1 static line for Codex |
| No TodoWrite / use bd remember | bento/CLAUDE.md; AGENTS.md codex block; bd prime | bd prime |
| Push-before-done protocol | bento/CLAUDE.md Session Completion; bd prime; land-work skill | Stop hook (stop-hook-check-unpushed) + land-work |
| Beads architecture (refs/dolt/data; jsonl passive export) | bento/CLAUDE.md only | keep, 1 line in bento/CLAUDE.md |
| Beads mutations from primary checkout | bento/AGENTS.md Issue tracking | keep, 1 line in bento/AGENTS.md |
| Repo purpose/map | bento/CLAUDE.md line 1; AGENTS.md Purpose + How-to-think | AGENTS.md |
| Don't hand-edit plugins//manifests; rebuild via build-plugins | AGENTS.md Rules + Safe editing + Maintenance workflow (x3) | AGENTS.md Rules, single bullet |
| Version management workflow | AGENTS.md Version Management (~225w); .claude/skills/version-bump.md | version-bump.md + 1-line pointer (after version-bump-enforcement hook lands) |

title: closure-scan mis-encodes Claude project dirs for paths containing dots, losing the session-liveness signal
description:
`catalog/skills/closure/scripts/closure-scan.py:465-470` (`_encode_claude_project_path`) only replaces `/` with `-`; Claude Code also encodes `.` as `-`. For worktrees under `~/.local/share/worktrees/...` — the default location documented by this very repo — the computed dir (`-home-...-.local-...`) never matches Claude's actual dir (`-home-...--local-...`), so `scan_claude_sessions` silently returns None and the Claude-session liveness verdict is missing exactly where it matters most. Removal-safety degrades to the weaker process/mtime signals without warning.

Work: replace both `/` and `.` (verify against a real `~/.claude/projects/` entry for a dotted path before finalizing); add a unit test with a dotted worktree path asserting the encoded name matches the real convention.

Acceptance: encoding test passes against a fixture mirroring an actual Claude projects dir name; closure-scan reports Claude liveness for a dotted-path worktree.

title: Repair, commit, and wire up the docs/stories corpus (currently untracked and stale)
description:
`docs/stories/` (20 storystore-schema stories + INDEX + README, generated in one batch 2026-05-24) is untracked, self-described as "the authoritative source for behavioral fidelity checks," and already stale: (a) `issue-completeness-precheck-fresh-review.md` was born under the skill's dead pre-rename name (renamed 3 days before generation in c1bfcb6) — title, slug, surface ref, and doc evidence all dangle; (b) `github-issue-lifecycle.md` asserts a quote using the dead name that no longer appears in `github-issue-flow/SKILL.md:44`; (c) `bentobug-capture-report.md` transcribes the stale "report writer hasn't shipped" claim; (d) `land-work-merge-and-cleanup.md` omits the mandatory independent code-review step added 2026-06-07 (cf6e4ba) and misattributes the Closure Evidence Rule (it lives in beads-issue-flow, not land-work). Also: all 20 `### Tests` evidence sections are empty despite `tests_applicable: true`; `.gitignore` lacks the `docs/stories/drift-todo.md` entry stories-init should have added; nothing in AGENTS.md points at the corpus.

Work (blocked on the intent-stories-storystore-decision issue): repair the four stale stories and regenerate INDEX.md; add test-evidence refs where tests exist or set tests_applicable false; commit the corpus; add the gitignore entry; add an AGENTS.md pointer. Wiring audits into landing is upstream storystore work (filed in storystore's tracker).

Acceptance: corpus tracked in git; the four stories match current skill behavior; INDEX regenerated; gitignore entry present.

title: Narrow auto-allow source-repo containment to plugin script directories
description:
`catalog/hooks/bento/claude/scripts/auto-allow.py` (~lines 131-164; codex peer `permission-request.py` ~130-162): `_find_source_repo_root` walks up to 8 ancestors for a marketplace root, then `_check_containment` accepts ANY script anywhere under that root ("via source repo"). When an agent works inside the bento source tree (or any clone), every `*.py` in the repo is auto-allowed for PreToolUse, not just the plugin's bundled helpers — broader than the docstring's claim. The shell-metachar hard-reject limits blast radius but the trust boundary is wrong.

Work: restrict source-repo containment to `plugins/<platform>/<name>/`, `catalog/skills/*/scripts/`, and `catalog/hooks/*/*/scripts/` under the discovered root; update both platform copies (the vendored-parity manifest should pin them); extend `tests/bento_auto_allow/` with a case asserting a repo-root-level stray script is NOT auto-allowed while a catalog skill script is.

Acceptance: new tests pass; existing auto-allow tests green; docstring matches the actual boundary.

title: swarm: clarify landing ownership, remove internal issue-ID leak, dedupe worktree rules, handle orphan script and config crash
description:
Four small defects in one skill: (1) Landing ambiguity — Phase 2 (~lines 178-181) says the teammate invokes `bento:land-work` as "the single landing path," while Phase 4 (~lines 210-214) tells the lead to "Land one completed branch at a time... resolve conflicts carefully"; two readings produce different actors running the merge. Add one sentence stating who executes land-work and that the lead serializes landings. (2) `SKILL.md:241` cites `bento-2xe`, a bento-repo-internal Beads ID that is unresolvable noise in consumer repos — replace with the actual semantics (exit 75 per the hook-script contract). (3) Worktree-placement prohibitions are restated in Phase 0 (~68-69), Phase 2 (~155-159), and `references/continuation-state.md` (~43-46) with wording drift; reference `launch-work/references/worktree-location.md` instead, as expedition does. (4) `scripts/swarm-state.py` is referenced by no markdown or script — document it in continuation-state.md or delete it; and `scripts/swarm-discover.py:33-35` raises an uncaught JSONDecodeError on malformed `swarm-config.json` where sibling discover scripts warn-and-continue — match that pattern.

Acceptance: landing actor unambiguous; no internal tracker IDs in catalog; worktree rules stated once; swarm-discover warns on malformed config (with test); swarm-state.py documented or removed. Rebuild/bump.

Post-review recommendation on item (1): the coherent reading of the current text is that the teammate executes bento:land-work (Phase 2 is explicit about it) and the lead authorizes landings one at a time without ever running the merge itself; encode that resolution unless the operator overrides.

title: Mechanically enforce version-bump evaluation at landing; tie versions to content
description:
Versioning is honor-system: nothing prevents landing a catalog change without a bump, the behavioral-vs-cosmetic call is pure agent judgment, and a deliberate "cosmetic, no bump" decision does not advance the baseline commit (only writing `catalog/plugin-versions.json` does — see `latest_version_commit` in scripts/bump-plugin-versions), so cosmetic paths reappear in `changed_paths` on every later evaluation. The ~225-word Version Management prose block in AGENTS.md exists to compensate and can shrink once enforcement is mechanical.

Work: (1) add a land-work pre hook-script in this repo (`land-work/hook-scripts/pre/NN-version-bump-check.sh`, using the documented extension point) that diffs `catalog/` and `scripts/build-plugins` against the merge base and fails when they changed but `catalog/plugin-versions.json` did not — with an explicit cosmetic-only override path that durably records the decision (e.g. a recorded baseline) so it does not re-fire forever; (2) optionally record a content hash per plugin alongside its version and fail the build when content changes without a bump; (3) shrink the AGENTS.md prose to a one-line pointer at `.claude/skills/version-bump.md` once the hook is exercised.

Acceptance: landing a catalog change without touching plugin-versions.json fails the pre-hook; a recorded cosmetic-only decision does not re-fire on the next landing; AGENTS.md block reduced.

Post-review concretization: implement the durable cosmetic-only override as a recorded baseline file (e.g. catalog/.version-baseline holding the evaluated commit SHA), written by the hook on explicit cosmetic-only confirmation and consulted alongside the plugin-versions.json commit when computing changed paths; scripts/bump-plugin-versions reads the same baseline. The content-hash idea (item 2 above) is OUT OF SCOPE for this issue — file separately if wanted.

Clarifications (second review pass): the baseline file is committed (it must survive across worktrees/sessions). Non-interactive confirmation flow: on failure the hook prints the exact remediation commands — either run scripts/bump-plugin-versions (behavioral) or run the hook's own --record-cosmetic mode (or a small helper) which writes the evaluated merge-base SHA to the baseline file for committing (cosmetic). Sub-step (3), shrinking the AGENTS.md prose, lands in the same change as the hook.

title: closure: deduplicate SKILL.md vs helper-output.md (~800 words) and fix contradictory branch-deletion rules
description:
closure is the largest skill in the catalog (2,688 words loaded per invocation) and duplicates its own reference doc nearly verbatim: SKILL.md says "See `closure/references/helper-output.md` for the branch-classification enum, liveness-verdict enum, apply-mode cleanup order, and recency calculation," then includes all four anyway (classification table ~ helper-output lines 3-12; liveness table + limitation paragraph ~ 14-35; recency ~ 120-127; correlation field list ~ 94-116) — roughly 700-900 duplicated words.

It also contradicts itself and land-work on manual deletion: the Safety section (~line 356) says "Never construct manual `git branch -D` or `git branch -d` commands" absolutely, while "When NOT to use" (~line 19) and "Routine post-landing cleanup" (~lines 96-102) prescribe exactly those commands, and an anti-rationalization row (~line 270) contradicts land-work step 10's mandated manual cleanup sequence.

Work: single-home each table/enum in helper-output.md, keeping workflow/safety/decision-matrix in SKILL.md; scope the deletion prohibition explicitly to "during a closure GC pass"; align the anti-rationalization row with land-work's boundary. Rebuild plugins; bump.

Acceptance: no table duplicated between the two files; the deletion rule is scoped; closure SKILL.md word count reduced at least 25%; no behavioral instruction lost (diff review against the decision matrix).

title: Add a doc-claims-vs-filesystem test pinning the highest-drift documentation facts
description:
Two refactors left 6+ docs asserting a stale world for months (deleted `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` still documented in four places; old plugins/ layout in three). A small declarative test can pin the claims that drift most, each re-derived from ground truth: (a) every path bullet in AGENTS.md "How to think about the repo" exists; (b) the plugin names listed in `docs/installing-plugins.md` equal the plugin set in `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`; (c) README's plugins-layout snippet matches the actual `plugins/` directory structure; (d) every pathspec in `.claude/skills/version-bump.md`'s documented `git add` line exists; (e) a forbidden-strings list (e.g. `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json`) appears in no current doc.

Keep the claim list small and explicit so the test does not become its own drift source. Depends on the doc-fix issues (purge-agents-manifest-claims, refresh-install-and-layout-docs) landing first.

Acceptance: test fails if any pinned claim regresses; passes on a fresh clone after the doc fixes.

title: Add a lint enforcing the hooks-read-cwd-from-stdin rule; retrofit existing hooks
description:
The AGENTS.md rule "Hook scripts must read the working directory from the `cwd` field in the stdin JSON payload, not from $PWD or the process CWD" was added 2026-05-20 (cdd8be3) as a 3-line doc edit with no retrofit sweep — and `ensure-worktree-permissions.py` (shipped 9 days earlier) violates it to this day (tracked separately). Prose rules about code shape demonstrably do not self-enforce here.

Work: add `scripts/check-hook-contract` (and a unit-test wrapper) that AST-walks every Python hook under `catalog/hooks/*/*/scripts/` and checks shell hooks where feasible: fail on `Path.cwd()`, `os.getcwd()`, or `$PWD`-as-project-dir usage unless the file carries an explicit `# hook-cwd-exempt: <reason>` annotation; additionally require that scripts reading stdin reference the payload's `cwd` field. Run it in the standard gate.

Acceptance: the lint fails on `ensure-worktree-permissions.py` before its fix and passes after; a synthetic violating hook fails the test.

title: Add a test that the checked-in plugins/ tree matches a fresh build of catalog/
description:
`tests/test_build_plugins.py` thoroughly tests the generator against temp copies, but nothing asserts the committed `plugins/` tree and generated manifests match a fresh build — the repo's central invariant per AGENTS.md ("do not hand-edit generated copies", "rebuild with scripts/build-plugins"). A manual review pass on 2026-06-09 verified the tree is currently in full sync (all 120 non-SKILL.md files byte-identical; 4 overlay-composed SKILL.md files match the compose function exactly), but the discipline is unenforced.

Work: add a test (or a `--check` mode on `scripts/build-plugins` invoked by a test) that builds into a temp dir and diffs against the repo's `plugins/` and `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json`, failing on any difference. The build is already deterministic (timestamp-free PNGs, stable JSON). Wire into the standard gate so it runs in build-plugins' TEST_CMD and in CI.

Acceptance: hand-editing any file under `plugins/` makes the test fail; a fresh clone passes.

title: Pin vendored duplicate scripts with a parity test; resolve the three git_state.py variants
description:
Skills vendor shared helpers and the copies have drifted: `git_state.py` exists in 5 skills — launch-work/handoff/expedition byte-identical, while land-work and swarm have each diverged (land-work's copy lacks `parse_worktrees`). Nothing records whether the forks are intentional. The telemetry trio (`bento_telemetry.py`, `record-bash.py`, `bento-telemetry.py`) is duplicated between `catalog/hooks/telemetry/claude/` and the codex peer (only a docstring differs), and `seed-agent-plugins.py` is identical across platforms — the codex copies have no tests of their own; parity is unpinned.

Work: (1) decide for each git_state.py fork whether divergence is intentional; (2) add `tests/test_vendored_parity.py` with an explicit manifest of path groups that must be byte-identical (or identical-modulo-docstring), where intentional forks must be listed with a reason string; (3) optionally, longer term, generate the copies from one canonical source at build time.

Acceptance: the parity test fails when any pinned group diverges; the manifest documents every intentional fork with a reason.

title: Fix dead cross-references: code-bloat-sniffer renamed-skill pointer; land-work broken reference link
description:
(1) `catalog/skills/code-bloat-sniffer/SKILL.md:137` directs handoff to `bento:issue-completeness-precheck`; that skill was renamed `issue-readiness-check` in commit c1bfcb6, which updated 27 files but missed this one (the backticked name wraps across a line break, defeating the grep sweep). (2) `catalog/skills/land-work/references/direct-primary-branch.md:28` points to `references/primary-branch-sync.md`, which exists only at `catalog/skills/closure/references/primary-branch-sync.md`.

Work: fix both references (for (2), either correct the relative path or copy/move the referenced doc — prefer whichever matches how skills resolve reference paths at runtime from the plugin cache); rebuild plugins; bump versions per the documented workflow.

Acceptance: grep for `issue-completeness-precheck` under catalog/ and plugins/ is empty; the land-work reference resolves to an existing file in the generated plugin layout. The companion skill-refs-linter issue prevents recurrence.

title: Remove stale claims that .agents/plugins/marketplace.json is generated (file deleted 2026-04-03)
description:
`.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` was deleted in commit 8c9d398 ("remove .agents; don't want local plugin installation"), and `scripts/build-plugins` has no Codex-marketplace generation code (the Codex manifest is generated client-side at install time by `install/_codex-installer-lib.sh`). Yet four places still document it as a generated artifact: `AGENTS.md:15` ("contains generated Codex marketplace metadata"), `AGENTS.md:90` ("Be careful in ... because it is generated"), `DESIGN.md:79` (listed as a build-plugins regeneration target), and `.claude/skills/version-bump.md:87`, whose documented `git add` line includes the path and therefore fails with a pathspec error — version bumps have happened for two months with every agent silently working around the failing documented command. `scripts/bump-plugin-versions:15-22` also lists it in IGNORED_PATHS (harmless residue).

Work: remove all four doc references; fix version-bump.md's `git add` line; optionally drop the IGNORED_PATHS entry; state in AGENTS.md/DESIGN.md that the Codex manifest is produced at install time by the installer.

Acceptance: grep for `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` finds no doc claims; the documented version-bump commit command runs without error.

title: Install/layout docs are stale: wrong plugins/ tree, 3-plugin list vs 7 published, wrong hooks-installation story
description:
Four related staleness families, best fixed in one sweep: (1) Layout: `README.md:242-257`, `DESIGN.md:60-67`, and `docs/installing-plugins.md:125-130` show `plugins/<plugin>/` containing both `.claude-plugin/` and `.codex-plugin/`; the real layout is platform-first (`plugins/claude/<plugin>/`, `plugins/codex/<plugin>/` — confirmed by `install/_codex-installer-lib.sh:73`). (2) Plugin list: `docs/installing-plugins.md:34-39`, `INSTALL.md:9-15`, and `DESIGN.md:38` say the marketplace publishes bento/trackers/stacks; `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` publishes 7 entries (adding session-id, intent-stories, and external bugshot/storystore), none of the extra four documented anywhere user-facing. (3) Hooks story: `docs/installing-plugins.md:157-163` and `INSTALL.md:35-36` claim hooks "are not installed through the plugin system" and "must be wired manually in ~/.claude/settings.json" with examples in hooks/README.md — but hooks ship inside plugins (`plugins/claude/bento/hooks/`, hook-only `session-id` plugin), and hooks/README.md contains no settings.json examples. (4) `DESIGN.md:201-203` "Hook format" describes the old top-level `hooks/<event>/` layout; `AGENTS.md:16` and `README.md:333` similarly describe `hooks/` as automation scripts when it now holds only docs/experiments.

Acceptance: tree diagrams match `ls plugins/`; documented plugin list matches marketplace.json; the hooks-installation section describes plugin-bundled hooks; DESIGN.md's stale section removed. (A companion issue adds a doc-claims test to keep these pinned.)

title: expedition close-task: documented invocation omits required --summary; failed merge leaves base worktree mid-conflict
description:
Two defects in the same command path: (1) `catalog/skills/expedition/SKILL.md:91` documents `close-task --expedition <name> [--branch <name>] --outcome kept|failed-experiment`, but `catalog/skills/expedition/scripts/expedition.py:554` declares `add_argument("--summary", required=True)` — the documented command fails with an argparse error at the most state-sensitive moment (landing under lease). (2) In `expedition.py` (~lines 377-387), when `git merge --no-ff` fails during `close-task --outcome kept`, the code releases the landing lease and returns an error but never runs `git merge --abort`, leaving the base worktree with MERGE_HEAD/conflict markers; the next close-task then fails its own `is_clean` gate. The prior rebase failure path has the same gap.

Work: add `--summary <text>` to the documented synopsis; on merge/rebase failure, abort the in-progress operation before returning so the worktree is clean and retryable; add a test that forces a conflicting merge and asserts the worktree is clean and the error payload structured.

Acceptance: documented command line satisfies the parser's required args; the new conflict test passes; existing expedition tests green. Rebuild/bump.

title: require-worktree hook blocks Write/Edit to paths outside the repo (e.g. /tmp) when session cwd is on main
description:
Observed 2026-06-10: with the session cwd at /home/ketan/project/bento on branch main, a Write tool call targeting `/tmp/issue-drafts/bento-drafts.md` was blocked by `~/.claude/hooks/bento/require-worktree.sh` ("Blocked: editing files directly on 'main' is not allowed"). The hook evaluates only the session cwd's branch, not the target file path, so it blocks legitimate writes to temp files, other repos, and anywhere else while the session sits on a protected branch. This breaks documented workflows that require writing temp files (e.g. the issue-readiness-check skill's draft/review files, handoff's /tmp output) and pushes agents toward bash-heredoc workarounds that bypass the hook entirely — weakening the control it implements.

Work: in `catalog/hooks/bento/claude/scripts/require-worktree.sh` (and its codex peer if any), extract the target file path from the tool input payload (`tool_input.file_path` for Edit/Write/NotebookEdit) and only block when the target path is inside the repo whose checked-out branch is protected. Keep blocking when the path is inside that repo. Add tests: write to /tmp allowed on main; write to repo file blocked on main; write to a different repo evaluated against that repo's branch.

Acceptance: temp-file writes succeed with cwd on main; in-repo writes remain blocked; tests cover both platforms' copies if both exist.

Current Code Facts (resolved 2026-06-10): the hook source is catalog/hooks/bento/claude/scripts/require-worktree.sh; it already parses the stdin payload and handles tool_input (see the STRIP_KEYS handling around lines 85-93), so the Edit/Write target file_path is available for a containment check. It is registered via a stable symlink under ~/.claude/hooks/bento/ maintained by register-require-worktree-hook.py, so a catalog fix plus rebuild propagates without re-registration. Reproduced twice on 2026-06-10, including by a fresh reviewer subagent writing its review file to /tmp.

title: Isolate e2e test runs from global claude config via fake HOME
description:
# Isolate e2e test runs from the developer's global claude configuration via fake HOME

## Summary

`E2ETestCase._run_claude()` in `tests/e2e_utils.py` inherits the developer's
real `HOME`. As a result, every test invocation of `claude` loads
`~/.claude/settings.json` (firing global hooks such as `bd prime`, tmux state,
and stop hooks) and any globally installed claude plugins.

The fix is to add `self.fake_home` to `setUp()` and inject it as `HOME` in
`_run_claude()`. Claude resolves `~/.claude/settings.json` and its plugin
directory from `HOME`; a blank temp home means no global settings or plugins
load. The scratch repo's `.claude/settings.json` still loads because claude
discovers it from the repo CWD, not `HOME`. Auth is supplied via
`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, which is already set.

Note: `_run_codex()` also exists in the harness but uses
`--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` to intentionally load global plugin hooks.
Codex isolation is a separate concern; this issue is claude-only.

## Scope

### In scope

**`tests/e2e_utils.py` changes:**
- In `setUp()` (line 46), after `self.root = Path(self.tmp.name).resolve()`
  (line 48), add:
  ```python
  self.fake_home = self.root / "home"
  self.fake_home.mkdir()
  ```
- In `_run_claude()` (line 209), inject `HOME` into the env before
  applying `extra_env`:
  ```python
  env["HOME"] = str(self.fake_home)
  ```
  Insert after `env["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"] = "sk-zolem-placeholder"` (line 218)
  and before the `if extra_env:` block (line 219). `extra_env` still wins so
  individual tests can override `HOME` if needed.

**`tests/session_id/test_session_start_e2e.py` changes:**
- Remove `self.temp_home = self.root / "home"` (line 23) and
  `self.temp_home.mkdir()` (line 25) from `setUp()` — the base class now
  provides `self.fake_home` at the same path (`self.root / "home"`).
- Keep `self.temp_tmp = self.root / "tmp"` and `self.temp_tmp.mkdir()` —
  TMPDIR is test-specific and stays in this test's `setUp()`.
- In both test methods (lines 48 and 57), drop `HOME` from the `extra_env`
  dict; keep `TMPDIR`:
  ```python
  # before
  extra_env={"HOME": str(self.temp_home), "TMPDIR": str(self.temp_tmp)}
  # after
  extra_env={"TMPDIR": str(self.temp_tmp)}
  ```
- Replace `self.temp_home` with `self.fake_home` at lines 49 and 58
  (the `session_id_file` path assertions).

### Out of scope
- `_run_codex()` — codex uses `CODEX_HOME` not `HOME`; intentionally uses
  `--dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` to load global plugin hooks; separate issue
- Any other change to zolem invocation, fixture setup, or test assertions

## Current code facts

- `E2ETestCase.setUp()` is at `tests/e2e_utils.py` line 46
- `self.root = Path(self.tmp.name).resolve()` is line 48;
  `self.tmp` is a `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory` cleaned up by
  `self.tmp.cleanup()` in `tearDown()` — `fake_home` as a subdir of
  `self.root` is therefore cleaned up automatically; no extra teardown needed
- `_run_claude()` env is built at lines 216–220; `HOME` is not currently set
- No other e2e test besides `test_session_start_e2e.py` passes `HOME` via
  `extra_env` to `_run_claude()` (confirmed by grep)
- `test_session_start_e2e.py` currently sets `self.temp_home = self.root /
  "home"` (line 23) — this is the same path the base class will use for
  `fake_home`, so no path changes are needed for the hook's output assertions

## Acceptance checks

- `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -t .` passes with no regressions
- After the change, the env dict passed to `subprocess.run` inside
  `_run_claude()` contains `HOME` pointing at a freshly created empty temp dir
- The session-start test still locates `session_id` under
  `self.fake_home / ".claude" / "session_id"` and scratch dir under
  `self.temp_tmp / "claude-session-<id>"`
- Running the e2e tests from a worktree with a dirty bento checkout does not
  fire any hooks from `~/.claude/settings.json`

title: land-work: add independent code review step before merge

title: Document Claude hook execution environment
description:
Research and document the actual execution environment that Claude Code hooks run in.

The environment to document:
- Environment variable inheritance (hooks DO inherit the full env from Claude's parent process, including user-defined vars like DOTFILES, PATH, TMUX, etc.)
- Working directory (always \$HOME, not project dir — must read cwd from JSON payload)
- stdin format (JSON payload, not TTY, not user input)
- stdout/stderr handling (specific JSON shapes vs arbitrary output)
- Claude-specific env vars injected by Claude Code (CLAUDECODE, CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, CLAUDE_CODE_TMPDIR, etc.)
- What env vars are NOT available (shell rc files not re-sourced, shell functions/aliases not available, virtualenvs only available if active when Claude launched)
- Process model (non-interactive shell, no TTY, subprocess of Claude Code)
- Adjacent invalid assumptions to debunk: exit 1 blocks, hooks run in project dir, NVM shims always available, shell functions usable

Target file: hooks/references/hook-environment.md

title: Document verified capabilities and environment model for agent hooks
description:
Title: Document verified capabilities and environment model for agent hooks

## Request

Evaluate and document what environment agent hooks and adjacent hook skills can rely on in the bento plugin workflow.

## Background

Another agent inferred that hooks do not inherit environment variables from the agent session that hosts them. This needs to be tested rather than accepted. The desired output is project documentation in bento describing the verified capabilities and limits of agent hooks.

## Scope

In scope:

- Inspect existing bento hook, skill, and lifecycle-extension documentation and implementation.
- Run experiments against the local hook runner and at least one real `codex` invocation.
- Verify whether hooks inherit custom environment variables from the host process, what current working directory they run with, what stdin payload is available, what `PATH` and `HOME` look like, and whether assumptions differ between hook scripts and hook skills.
- Explore nearby assumptions likely to be wrong or underspecified, such as relying on process cwd, repo root discovery, writable paths, shell initialization, and command availability.
- Add or update bento documentation with the experimentally verified behavior and caveats.

Out of scope:

- Changing hook behavior unless a small documentation-supporting fixture or test is clearly needed.
- Reworking the plugin build system beyond any required docs regeneration.
- Closing tracker work before the documentation branch has landed.

## Acceptance Checks

- The work includes notes or evidence from actual local experiments, including a real `codex` CLI run.
- The documentation states which claims are verified, which are runtime-specific, and which assumptions remain unsafe.
- The documentation distinguishes bento lifecycle extension hook scripts from agent-runtime hooks and hook skills where relevant.
- If canonical skill files or build scripts are touched, version-bump requirements are evaluated and generated plugin output is rebuilt when required.
- The closest relevant verification commands pass, or any inability to run them is documented.

title: require-worktree hook: allow markdown file writes on main
description:
# require-worktree hook: allow markdown file writes on main

## Summary

The `require-worktree` hook blocks all file edits directly on `main`. This is
correct for source code, but it also blocks writing markdown files (`.md`,
`.markdown`), which is often legitimate — e.g. plan files, specs, notes,
ad-hoc docs — and should not require a feature branch.

Add an exemption: when `tool_name` is `Write` or `Edit` and
`tool_input.file_path` ends with `.md` or `.markdown`, allow the call through
instead of blocking it.

## Current Code Facts

- Hook source: `catalog/hooks/bento/claude/scripts/require-worktree.sh`
  (canonical) and its installed copy at
  `~/.claude/hooks/bento/require-worktree.sh`
- The hook reads the full Claude Code PreToolUse stdin payload into
  `$payload_raw` via `payload_raw="$(cat 2>/dev/null || true)"`.
- `tool_name` is available at `d.get('tool_name')` in the Python inline block.
- `tool_input` is available at `d.get('tool_input', {})`. For `Write` the
  relevant key is `file_path`; for `Edit` it is also `file_path`.
- The blocking logic begins after the `.agent-mode.local` opt-out check at
  line 52.
- The opt-out mechanism (`require_worktree=false` in `.agent-mode.local`) is a
  repo-level escape hatch and is intentionally coarser than per-tool exemptions.

## Desired Behavior

- `Write` or `Edit` targeting a `.md` or `.markdown` file on `main` → allowed,
  no block.
- All other file types on `main` → blocked as before.
- `Bash` calls on `main` → blocked as before (no `file_path` key).
- The exemption check must happen in bash (before the Python logging block),
  so that no rejection log entry is written for allowed markdown writes.

## Acceptance Checks

- [ ] Write a `.md` file on `main` → hook exits 0, file is written.
- [ ] Edit a `.md` file on `main` → hook exits 0, edit proceeds.
- [ ] Write a `.py` file on `main` → hook exits 2, blocked.
- [ ] Edit a `.ts` file on `main` → hook exits 2, blocked.
- [ ] `Bash` call on `main` → hook exits 2, blocked.
- [ ] Existing unit tests for the hook continue to pass.
- [ ] `scripts/build-plugins` regenerates the installed copy cleanly.
- [ ] Version bump evaluation performed per CLAUDE.md policy.

## In Scope

- Exemption logic in `catalog/hooks/bento/claude/scripts/require-worktree.sh`
- Corresponding unit tests
- Plugin rebuild + version bump if warranted

## Out of Scope

- Codex `permission-request.py` (different hook, separate concern)
- Any other file-extension exemptions
- Changes to the `.agent-mode.local` opt-out mechanism

## Resolved Lookups (pre-filing)

- **Line reference drift**: The `.agent-mode.local` opt-out block ends with
  `exit 0` inside the `while` loop; locate it by pattern
  (`require_worktree.*false.*exit 0`) rather than by line number.
- **Exemption insertion point**: The bash exemption check goes immediately
  after the closing `fi` of the `.agent-mode.local` config block and before
  the `cat >&2 <<'MESSAGE'` block that emits the rejection message. Inserting
  there means no rejection log entry is written for allowed markdown writes.
- **Do not edit the installed copy directly**: Only edit
  `catalog/hooks/bento/claude/scripts/require-worktree.sh`. The installed
  copy at `~/.claude/hooks/bento/require-worktree.sh` is regenerated by
  `scripts/build-plugins` and must not be hand-edited.

title: Remove stale launch-work progress-log instructions or restore the helper
description:
Title: Remove stale launch-work progress-log instructions or restore the helper

Description:
The `launch-work` skill currently instructs agents to run
`launch-work/scripts/launch-work-log.py`, but that helper no longer exists in
the repository or generated plugin copies. Agents following the skill hit a
dead command during normal worktree setup.

Current evidence:
- `catalog/skills/launch-work/SKILL.md` references
  `launch-work/scripts/launch-work-log.py init`.
- The same skill also says agents must not proceed past checkpoints without
  writing the log through `launch-work-log.py update`.
- `plugins/claude/bento/skills/launch-work/SKILL.md` and
  `plugins/codex/bento/skills/launch-work/SKILL.md` contain the same generated
  stale references.
- `catalog/skills/launch-work/scripts/` contains bootstrap, verify, and
  lifecycle-extension helpers, but no `launch-work-log.py`.
- Git history shows commit `757495a` removed launch and land work logging,
  including `launch-work-log.py` and its tests.
- A later refactor commit appears to have reintroduced progress-log wording in
  `SKILL.md` without restoring the helper.

Desired change:
Make the `launch-work` workflow internally consistent again. Either:
- remove the stale progress-log instructions and non-negotiable log rules from
  the canonical `catalog/skills/launch-work/SKILL.md`, then rebuild generated
  plugin outputs; or
- intentionally restore `launch-work-log.py` plus appropriate tests and
  generated outputs.

Given the earlier removal commit was explicit, removing the stale references is
probably the smaller and more consistent fix unless product direction says
progress logging should return.

Likely in scope:
- Update canonical `catalog/skills/launch-work/SKILL.md`.
- Rebuild generated plugin outputs with `scripts/build-plugins`.
- Add or update tests if a helper is restored.
- Evaluate the required plugin version bump because this touches canonical
  skill behavior.

Out of scope:
- Changing branch/worktree creation behavior.
- Reworking lifecycle hook-script or hook-skill behavior.
- Changing `land-work` merge semantics.

Acceptance checks:
- No generated or canonical `launch-work` skill instructs agents to run a
  missing helper.
- `scripts/build-plugins` succeeds and regenerated plugin outputs match the
  canonical source.
- The version-bump workflow required by `AGENTS.md` has been performed and any
  required bump is included.

title: Make launch-work and land-work mandatory in project-level docs
description:
Title: Make launch-work and land-work mandatory in project-level docs

Description:
Agents can discover strong lifecycle requirements inside the `launch-work` and
`land-work` skill files, but the project-level documentation and plugin
metadata do not make those requirements equally explicit. This makes it easier
for an agent to read the repository entry points and understand the skills as
available workflow tools or extension-point systems rather than mandatory gates.

Current evidence:
- `catalog/skills/launch-work/SKILL.md` declares `launch-work` as a hard
  trigger before any code or file edits.
- `catalog/skills/land-work/SKILL.md` declares `land-work` as a hard trigger
  after finishing approved feature-branch work.
- Generated Claude and Codex copies preserve those hard-trigger descriptions.
- `AGENTS.md` and `README.md` primarily mention `launch-work` and `land-work`
  in the context of extension points.
- `DESIGN.md` describes them functionally, but does not present them as
  required lifecycle gates.
- `plugins/codex/bento/.codex-plugin/plugin.json` advertises Bento generally;
  its default prompts do not mention `launch-work` or `land-work`.

Desired change:
Update the project-level documentation and plugin-facing metadata so a fresh
agent sees the expected lifecycle clearly before implementation work begins:
- use `launch-work` before any repository artifact edit, including small docs
  or config changes;
- use `land-work` after completing approved feature-branch work;
- do not edit or land directly from `main` except for explicitly approved
  branch-management tasks such as merges or rebases.

Likely in scope:
- Add a short "Required Agent Lifecycle" section or equivalent wording to
  `AGENTS.md`.
- Add corresponding user-facing guidance to `README.md` and, if appropriate,
  `DESIGN.md`.
- Update generated-plugin source metadata in `scripts/build-plugins`
  (`PLUGIN_DEFS["bento"]`, including `long_description` and/or
  `default_prompts`) so the Bento plugin description or default prompts surface
  `launch-work` and `land-work` as the standard lifecycle, then rebuild
  generated plugin outputs with `scripts/build-plugins`.
- Evaluate whether the change warrants a plugin version bump because it touches
  skill/plugin behavior or generated plugin metadata.

Out of scope:
- Changing the behavior of the `launch-work` or `land-work` workflows.
- Reworking branch, worktree, or landing helper scripts.
- Closing any existing worktree cleanup gaps.

Acceptance checks:
- A fresh agent reading project entry points can tell that `launch-work` is the
  required pre-edit path and `land-work` is the required post-completion path.
- The docs distinguish mandatory lifecycle usage from the extension-point
  mechanism for adding hooks.
- Generated plugin outputs are consistent with canonical sources after any
  source or build-script changes.
- The version-bump evaluation required by `AGENTS.md` has been performed and
  any required bump is included with regenerated outputs.
